compiled_state.title: sprint-list-epics.sh reports 'No open epics found' despite open epics existing (3rd investigation)
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 5+escalation (ADVANCED). Script exits 0 with correct output when called directly, but sprint saw exit 1 'No open epics found'. Prior fixes: 93a6c760 (find -L symlinks), 57ab5a0e (rename count var), b798cb97 (retry logic). None resolved the issue.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Investigation conclusion: Transient environmental failure. No code defect found. Script runs reliably 5/5 times. Plugin cache and worktree are identical (same commit c8afbd5). Tracker dir resolves correctly through symlink (425 entries, 40 open epics, 0 reducer errors). Three prior fix attempts addressed race conditions already ruled out. No code change needed — self-resolved.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: RECLASSIFIED: Reproducible. Fails reliably when /sprint is the first command in a session. Works on subsequent invocations. Not a race condition. Session-state-dependent. Escalating to ADVANCED investigation (score: 7 = severity:1 + complexity:2 + environment:0 + prior_fixes:+2 + cascading:+2).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: ROOT CAUSE (5th attempt): sprint-list-epics.sh reads the tracker dir directly without ensuring it is initialized. In a fresh worktree, the tracker symlink does not exist until the first ticket command runs. Retry logic only covers tracker-exists-but-reducer-fails. Fix: add init call before reading.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: added ticket-init.sh call to sprint-list-epics.sh when tracker dir missing and TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR not set. Root cause: script bypassed the ticket dispatcher _ensure_initialized() that creates the worktree symlink. Found same anti-pattern in sprint-next-batch.sh (cb6a-8b22) and purge-non-project-tickets.sh (126b-369d).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
